description: Guide for choosing the right ExtendSim simulation type based on the system being modeled

continuous.description: Models systems where values change continuously over time (differential equations, feedback loops)
useWhen: Variables change smoothly over time (temperature, population, chemical concentration)
System is described by differential equations or rate-of-change relationships
No individual entities/items need to be tracked

keyBlocks.name: Lookup Table
keyBlocks.purpose: Maps input values to output values

discrete.description: Models systems where individual items (customers, parts, orders) move through a process with queues and activities
useWhen: Individual entities (customers, parts, packets, orders) flow through the system
Entities wait in queues for processing
Activities/processes take variable amounts of time
System involves routing, branching, or merging of entity paths

keyBlocks.name: Exit
keyBlocks.purpose: Removes items from the system and collects statistics

flow.description: Models systems with continuous flow of material through discrete steps (tanks, valves, conveyors with flow rates)
useWhen: Material flows as a continuous stream (not individual items) but through discrete process steps
Tanks/buffers fill and drain at varying rates
Flow can be constrained by capacity limits or valves
Hybrid system: combines item-based and flow-based behavior

keyBlocks.name: Tank
keyBlocks.purpose: Stores flow material (capacity, fill level, max rates)

keyBlocks.name: Merge
keyBlocks.purpose: Combines multiple flow streams into one

keyBlocks.name: Diverge
keyBlocks.purpose: Splits one flow stream into multiple

rbd.description: Reliability Block Diagrams - models system reliability with failure/repair cycles
useWhen: Components have failure rates and repair times
Need to calculate system MTBF, MTTR, or availability

keyBlocks.name: Shutdown
keyBlocks.purpose: Models failure/repair cycles with TBF and TTR distributions

keyBlocks.name: Shift
keyBlocks.purpose: Models scheduled on/off cycles (shifts, maintenance windows)

decisionGuide.description: Answer these questions to determine the right simulation type

questions.question: Does the system involve individual entities (customers, parts, orders) that move through steps?
Are the entities processed individually (one at a time) or as continuous flow (bulk/stream)?
Does the system need flow rates (liters/min, tons/hour) and tank levels?
questions.yes: Use FLOW simulation (Rate.lbr blocks) - requires Pro license
questions.no: Use DISCRETE simulation with high throughput settings

decisionGuide.hybrid_note: Many real systems combine types. ExtendSim Pro can mix Item, Rate, and Value blocks in one model. For example, a bottling plant might use Rate blocks for liquid flow and Item blocks for individual bottles.
